---
title: "Social Media Ad Campaign Performance"
subtitle: "A Cross-Platform Snapshot: Where Is Our Ad Spend Paying Off?"
author: "Serena Shi"
date: today
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    nav-buttons: [linkedin, twitter]
    scrolling: false
logo: ""
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(scales)

# Load data (download from Kaggle or use simulated version below)
set.seed(42)
n <- 500

platforms  <- c("Facebook","Instagram","Pinterest","Twitter")
camp_types <- c("Awareness","Consideration","Conversion","Retargeting")
targets    <- c("18-24","25-34","35-44","45-54","55+")

df <- tibble(
  date           = sample(seq(as.Date("2024-01-01"), as.Date("2024-12-31"), by="day"), n, replace=TRUE),
  platform       = sample(platforms,  n, replace=TRUE, prob=c(0.35,0.30,0.15,0.20)),
  campaign_type  = sample(camp_types, n, replace=TRUE),
  target_age     = sample(targets,    n, replace=TRUE),
  spend          = round(runif(n, 100, 5000), 2),
  impressions    = round(runif(n, 5000, 500000)),
  clicks         = round(runif(n, 50, 15000)),
  conversions    = round(runif(n, 5, 800)),
  revenue        = round(runif(n, 500, 50000), 2)
) |>
  mutate(
    ctr  = clicks / impressions,
    cvr  = conversions / clicks,
    cpc  = spend / clicks,
    roas = revenue / spend,
    roi  = (revenue - spend) / spend * 100
  )

platform_summary <- df |>
  group_by(platform) |>
  summarise(
    total_spend       = sum(spend),
    total_revenue     = sum(revenue),
    avg_roas          = mean(roas),
    avg_ctr           = mean(ctr),
    avg_cvr           = mean(cvr),
    total_conversions = sum(conversions),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

campaign_summary <- df |>
  group_by(campaign_type) |>
  summarise(
    avg_roi  = mean(roi),
    avg_roas = mean(roas),
    avg_ctr  = mean(ctr),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

# Overview

## Row {height=20%}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Ad Spend"
list(
  icon  = "cash-coin",
  color = "primary",
  value = dollar(sum(df$spend))
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Revenue"
list(
  icon  = "graph-up-arrow",
  color = "success",
  value = dollar(sum(df$revenue))
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Avg ROAS"
list(
  icon  = "percent",
  color = "info",
  value = paste0(round(mean(df$roas), 2), "x")
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Conversions"
list(
  icon  = "check2-circle",
  color = "warning",
  value = comma(sum(df$conversions))
)
```
## Row {height=80%}

```{r}
#| title: ROAS by Platform
p <- platform_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(platform, avg_roas), y = avg_roas, fill = platform)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(avg_roas, 2), "x")),
            hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Return on Ad Spend (ROAS)",
       title = "Which platform delivers the best return?",
       subtitle = "ROAS > 1 means revenue exceeds spend") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("x","y"))
```

```{r}
#| title: Click-Through Rate by Campaign Type
p2 <- campaign_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(campaign_type, avg_ctr), y = avg_ctr, fill = campaign_type)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(avg_ctr, accuracy=0.01)),
            hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy=0.1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Avg Click-Through Rate (CTR)",
       title = "Which campaign type earns the most clicks?",
       subtitle = "Higher CTR = more engaging ad creative") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
ggplotly(p2, tooltip = c("x","y"))
```

# Platform Deep Dive

## Row

```{r}
#| title: Spend vs Revenue by Platform
p3 <- platform_summary |>
  pivot_longer(c(total_spend, total_revenue), names_to="metric", values_to="amount") |>
  mutate(metric = recode(metric, total_spend="Spend", total_revenue="Revenue")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x=platform, y=amount, fill=metric)) +
  geom_col(position="dodge", width=0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=dollar_format()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("Spend"="#E74C3C","Revenue"="#2ECC71")) +
  labs(x=NULL, y="Amount (USD)", fill=NULL,
       title="Ad Spend vs Revenue by Platform",
       subtitle="Green bars should always exceed red bars for profitability") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=12) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(face="bold"))
ggplotly(p3, tooltip=c("x","y","fill"))
```

```{r}
#| title: Conversion Rate by Platform
p4 <- platform_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x=platform, y=avg_cvr, fill=platform)) +
  geom_col(show.legend=FALSE, width=0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=avg_cvr*0.9, ymax=avg_cvr*1.1), width=0.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=percent_format(accuracy=0.1)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Set1") +
  labs(x=NULL, y="Avg Conversion Rate (CVR)",
       title="Which platform converts the best?",
       subtitle="CVR = conversions ÷ clicks") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=12) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(face="bold"))
ggplotly(p4, tooltip=c("x","y"))
```

# ROI Analysis

## Row

```{r}
#| title: ROI Distribution by Campaign Type
p5 <- df |>
  ggplot(aes(x=campaign_type, y=roi, fill=campaign_type)) +
  geom_violin(alpha=0.7, show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width=0.1, fill="white", outlier.shape=NA) +
  labs(x=NULL, y="ROI (%)",
       title="ROI Spread by Campaign Type",
       subtitle="Wider violin = more variability in returns") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=12) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(face="bold"))
ggplotly(p5, tooltip=c("x","y"))
```

```{r}
#| title: Monthly Spend Trend
p6 <- df |>
  mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) |>
  group_by(month, platform) |>
  summarise(spend = sum(spend), .groups="drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x=month, y=spend, color=platform, group=platform)) +
  geom_line(linewidth=1) +
  geom_point(size=2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=dollar_format()) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette="Set2") +
  labs(x=NULL, y="Monthly Spend (USD)", color="Platform",
       title="Ad Spend Over Time by Platform",
       subtitle="Identify budget allocation shifts month-over-month") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=12) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(face="bold"))
ggplotly(p6, tooltip=c("x","y","color"))
```

# Data Table

## Row

```{r}
#| title: Campaign-Level Data
df |>
  select(date, platform, campaign_type, target_age,
         spend, impressions, clicks, conversions, revenue, roas, roi) |>
  arrange(desc(date)) |>
  mutate(
    spend       = dollar(spend),
    revenue     = dollar(revenue),
    impressions = comma(impressions),
    clicks      = comma(clicks),
    roas        = round(roas, 2),
    roi         = paste0(round(roi,1), "%")
  ) |>
  datatable(
    filter    = "top",
    options   = list(pageLength=15, scrollX=TRUE),
    rownames  = FALSE,
    caption   = "Individual campaign records — use filters above each column to drill down"
  )
```
